title: A.3 inspector plane end-to-end smoke (HTTP + IPC + TraceProjection + replay-drive)
goal: Run the full inspector-plane pipeline end-to-end against a live KERNEL-004 build: start server, query read endpoints via HTTP, query identical read endpoints via Tauri IPC, fetch a TraceProjection for a synthetic session, perform one replay-drive via signed envelope, confirm an EventLedger row is emitted, and assert no compile-leak of mutation paths into the read-only trait.
expected_diff_shape: New integration test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/a3_inspector_smoke.rs spinning up a synthetic session + the inspector server + a CDP-driven Diagnostics-panel mock; new gov-check sub-check; run produces a smoke report JSONL.
implementation_notes: End-to-end wire test for AC-INSPECTOR-PLANE-BIND + READONLY + MUTATION-ROUTING + HBR-VIS-004. Flow: (1) Start handshake_core in test mode with `inspector` feature; (2) Inject a synthetic session into EventLedger (one of each event kind covering TraceProjection's seven fields); (3) Start InspectorServer (MT-029) and capture port; (4) HTTP GETs for all read endpoints — assert payloads parse and TraceProjection has all seven fields populated; (5) Invoke equivalent Tauri IPC commands (MT-032) — assert identical payloads byte-for-byte; (6) POST a valid replay-drive envelope (MT-030) — assert 200 + KernelActionCatalogV1 dispatch + EventLedger row emitted; (7) POST an extra-field body — assert 403; (8) Verify no compile-leak: run a direct rustc compile-check against a synthetic test crate that tries to call a write-side method through the InspectorReadV1 trait and assert it fails to compile. Output JSONL `{ endpoints_passed, ipc_passed, trace_projection_fields, replay_drive_success, compile_leak_detected: false }`. Justfile recipe `hbr-inspector-smoke` wired into gov-check umbrella. The smoke test fails loud on any of the six conditions.
risk_if_missed: Without the smoke, individual A.3 MTs prove pieces but not integration; a regression that breaks the chain ships green.
validator_focus: HTTP and IPC payloads byte-identical; direct rustc compile-leak test confirms read-only boundary holds at compile time; smoke fails on any of six conditions.

minimum_controls: direct rustc compile-leak test confirms the read-only boundary holds at compile time (not just runtime).
HTTP and IPC payloads are byte-identical for the same query (no schema drift between transports).
Smoke fails loud on any of the six conditions.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: End-to-end smoke spins up the inspector server, hits all read endpoints, asserts HTTP bytes equal serde-IPC bytes, runs a valid replay-drive plus an extra-field 403 attempt, and a separate rustc compile-leak test confirms the read-only boundary holds at compile time.
